label: GEOS XML authoring as eval target (36-task easy-setting suite)
rationale: Task set mined from GEOS tutorial RSTs; easy setting provides all values; ground truth XML available. Well-established by prior repo1/repo2/repo3 work.

label: XMLTreeSim as headline metric (0-1, reported as x10)
rationale: Replaces arbitrary weighted composite; respects tree structure; handles element multiplicity via bipartite matching; per-section sub-scores fall out naturally. Implemented in src/eval/judge_geos.py.

label: Docker-per-task sandbox + hardlink contamination filter + RAG blocklist
rationale: Two-layer enforcement: filesystem-level hardlink tree bind-mounted ro + MCP-server-level excluded-source filter. Variant stem expansion + example_pairs.jsonl RST mapping. Audited via eval_metadata.json per task.

label: GEOS-RAG plugin (3-DB ChromaDB + geos-rag skill) as candidate adaptation surface
rationale: Custom RAG ported from repo1/repo2 with 3 indices: documentation (RST), XML files, schema (input-schema + datastructure definitions). Per-task ChromaDB copy + MCP preflight. Whether this helps CC vs. CC's built-in file browsing is the open empirical question.

label: ablation_deepseek_v2: no-plugin CC + deepseek-v3.2 on 36 tasks
rationale: Apples-to-apples no-plugin baseline using the v2 harness (primer-in-system-prompt, --disallowedTools Skill). 33/36 success, 3 timeout.
decision_reason: Serves as the reference no-plugin condition for all plugin-vs-baseline contrasts on deepseek-v3.2.

label: repo3_eval_run2: plugin CC + minimax-m2.7 on 36 tasks
rationale: Earlier plugin run scored at 0.809 mean TreeSim, 78%% at >=7. Model differs from ablation baseline so this alone does NOT isolate plugin contribution.
decision_reason: Useful as plugin-with-stronger-model reference point, but confounded vs E01.

label: RQ: find CC adaptation that beats vanilla CC at model parity
rationale: Paper contribution rests on demonstrating some adaptation wins. First order of business for this session.

label: repo3_eval_run4: plugin CC + deepseek-v3.2 on 36 tasks
rationale: Apples-to-apples vs E01: same 36 tasks, same deepseek-v3.2 model, same 20-min timeout. Scored 2026-04-20 against experiments_gt. 1/36 failed scoring (ExampleSPE11b parse error). Paired comparison on common 35 tasks shows plugin wins on 29/35, loses on 6, mean delta +0.175, pass >=0.7 rate 88.6% vs 54.3%.
decision_reason: Headline positive result for the paper: custom RAG plugin demonstrably improves CC on the easy GEOS-XML task at model parity. Resolves the main gap flagged in ablation_findings.md.

label: Memory-system adaptation (Mem0 / Cheatsheet / BoT / GMemory; frozen pre-learned preferred)
rationale: User's strongest bet. Frozen pre-learned favored over continual because it preserves parallelism. Can be implemented external-to-CC (prompt-prepended cheatsheet) or as an MCP tool.

label: Primer design variations (structure, scope, in-prompt vs workspace file)
rationale: Ablation findings soft-suggest primer-in-system-prompt reduces timeouts. Instruction/structure variations untested. Bordering on vanilla-CC /init behavior so 'novelty' is thin — useful as a supporting result only.

label: File-tree context injection (pre-computed GEOS tree in system prompt)
rationale: Empirically CC wastes turns on ls/glob in inputFiles and docs. A static tree listing in the system prompt could cut those tool calls.

label: tree_run1: plugin + precomputed inputFiles tree (~4500 tokens) on 17 test tasks
rationale: Injected a compact index of 746 XML files across 87 subdirs of /geos_lib/inputFiles into system prompt. Hypothesis: removes Glob/Bash-find exploration burn; gives agent direct file references. Result: 17/17 scored (3 timeouts produced partial XML that scored), 0 redacted_thinking failures. Mean TreeSim 0.604 vs plugin-only 0.831 paired, delta -0.227. Loses on 15/17 tasks. Largest losses on EXACTLY the tasks plugin-only rescues most (Sneddon -0.719; DPWellbore -0.687; Mandel -0.637). Filetree seems to distract agent from RAG-driven workflow.
decision_reason: System-prompt injection of any auxiliary content (cheatsheet OR filetree) hurts plugin. Plugin's value is specifically the RAG MCP + skill prompt; further system-prompt augmentation is counterproductive. Strong cross-experiment pattern.

label: minprimer_run1: plugin + minimal primer (~450 tokens vs full ~10K) on 17 test tasks
rationale: Primer ablation: does primer content carry weight. Result: DECISIVELY YES. Minimal primer scored mean 0.596 vs full-primer plugin 0.831 paired delta -0.235. 16/17 tasks regress. 4 timeouts (vs 0 in E03). Largest losses on same 3 tasks as E07 (Sneddon -0.723, DPWellbore -0.688, Mandel -0.677). Primer content is load-bearing — the plugin's value is only fully realized with detailed primer context. Full primer + RAG MCP together form the active-ingredient stack.
decision_reason: Minimal primer is strictly worse. Full primer is load-bearing. Surprising finding: even with RAG present, the primer's detailed documentation map and schema-navigation context are needed for the rescue mechanism to fire on weak-model tasks.

label: memws_run1: plugin + abstract cheatsheet in /workspace/CHEATSHEET.md (NOT system prompt) on 17 test tasks
rationale: Delivery-channel test: cheatsheet_abstract.md (550 tokens; shortcuts + pitfalls) copied to /workspace/CHEATSHEET.md; system prompt has only a 2-line pointer. Result: 11/17 agent success (vs 17/17 E03), 6 timeouts, 1 ParseError, 1 FileNotFoundError. Paired 15 scored: memws 0.622 vs plugin-only 0.831 delta -0.212. Wins 2/15, loses 13/15. Same rescue-task regressions as E07/E08 (Sneddon -0.733, DPWellbore -0.696, Mandel -0.618). Workspace-file delivery is NOT a safe channel for memory on this stack.
decision_reason: Channel distinction doesn't help. The failure is deeper than delivery — perturbing the tightly-coupled primer+plugin state consistently breaks the rescue mechanism on the same 3 tasks. Only remaining memory channel to test: MCP tool (E11).

label: Simpler flat-chunking RAG as alternative to the 3-DB custom design
rationale: User's own note: maybe the 3-DB design was overengineered; plain chunked doc RAG might be a fairer 'standard RAG' ablation point.

label: Memory-on-top-of-plugin: stack frozen pre-learned cheatsheet over existing plugin (demonstrates adaptation stacking)
rationale: E03 shows plugin already wins. Next research question: does adding memory yield compounding gains? Design: hold out N tasks as train set, CC+plugin writes lessons-learned cheatsheet, freeze cheatsheet, evaluate on remaining test tasks with cheatsheet prepended to system prompt. Preserves parallelism.

label: mem_run1: plugin + cheatsheet CC + deepseek-v3.2 on 17 test tasks
rationale: First memory-stacking experiment. Cheatsheet (~1700 tokens, 6 themed sections) generated from 18 held-out train trajectories of repo3_eval_run4 via deepseek lesson-extraction + aggregation. Test set: 17 paired tasks. Result: MEMORY DECISIVELY HURT PERFORMANCE. 13/17 success (vs 17/17 in plugin baseline); 3 tasks failed with no XML output, 1 timed out. On 14 paired-scorable tasks, memory mean TreeSim 0.532 vs plugin 0.831 (delta -0.322). Memory wins on 1/14, loses on 13/14. Including 3 no-output tasks as 0, overall mean 0.438 (delta -0.393). Dominant failure mode: agent emits 'redacted_thinking' block then end_turn after 3-6 turns without calling Write/Edit. Hypothesis: cheatsheet bloat past ~23K system-prompt tokens triggers premature stop in deepseek-v3.2, and task-specific advice (e.g., 'use InternalWellbore') from train-task patterns mismatches test-task requirements.
decision_reason: Frozen-cheatsheet approach as specified in D-001 does not work on deepseek-v3.2. The hypothesis of 'adaptation stacking' is NOT supported by this design. Dead end for cheatsheet-in-system-prompt; revisit memory via (a) shorter cheatsheet restricted to cross-task patterns only, (b) memory as MCP tool rather than system-prompt inject, (c) stronger model that handles longer context.

label: Plugin decomposition: geos-rag skill vs MCP RAG server attribution
rationale: Current plugin bundles (a) geos-rag skill prompt/instructions and (b) 3-DB ChromaDB MCP server. To attribute the E03 gain, need to run plugin-without-RAG-MCP (skill only) and RAG-MCP-without-skill. Defer to week-scope unless time.

label: gmem_run1: plugin + G-Memory-lite MCP tool (memory_lookup) on 17 test tasks
rationale: Frozen memory index of 18 train trajectories delivered as an MCP tool memory_lookup(query). Agent called the tool on 17/17 tasks (1 call each) — tool is discovered and used. Paired delta -0.192 vs plugin-only (0.638 vs 0.831 on 17). 13/17 success, 4 timeouts, wins 1/17 (pkn +0.142), loses 16/17. DIFFERENT from other negatives: memory RESCUES DPWellbore (0.939 vs plugin 0.992, only -0.05) and IMPROVES pkn. But still fails Sneddon -0.716, Mandel -0.669. Smallest negative delta in the memory battery (-0.192 vs -0.212 to -0.322 for E04/E05/E07/E08/E09).
decision_reason: MCP-tool channel is the least-bad memory variant but still net negative on the 17-task test subset. The rescue mechanism on Sneddon + Mandel appears fragile to any perturbation regardless of delivery channel. Memory-via-MCP is not strictly dead - it DOES rescue some tasks where other memory variants fail - but overall hurts.

label: gmem_v2_run1: gated memory_lookup (threshold=0.6 + delay-trigger system-prompt) on 17 test tasks
rationale: Tested XN-009's diagnosis. Result: paired delta -0.186 (vs E11 -0.192) on 16 scored tasks (Sneddon failed_no_outputs - new failure mode where agent asked user a question via AskUserQuestion). Marginal improvement over E11. 4 wins (pkn +0.221 best), 12 losses; rescue tasks Mandel/DPWellbore/DeviatedElastic still catastrophic. Threshold correctly suppressed memory on Sneddon and Mandel, but agent still failed - suggests the memory INSTRUCTION (375 tokens) itself anchors behavior, even when memory is not called.
decision_reason: Threshold + delay alone insufficient. Hypothesis: the system-prompt instruction about memory (even cautionary) anchors agent reasoning. E13 tests this by stripping the instruction entirely (memory tool still available via MCP).

label: gmemsilent_run1: memory MCP tool available with NO system-prompt instruction on 17 test tasks
rationale: Memory tool present, agent never called it (mem=0 on all 17). Paired delta -0.180 vs E03 (0.651 vs 0.831). 5 timeouts. Result essentially identical to E11/E12, suggesting either tool-list presence affects behavior or this is seed variance. E14 (plain plugin re-run) confirmed it's the latter.
decision_reason: Same magnitude regression as E11/E12. Motivated E14 plugin-only seed-2 variance check, which showed plain plugin also drops to 0.616 — variance, not memory.

label: plugin_seed2: PLAIN plugin-only on 17 test (variance check, fresh seed)
rationale: Same plugin, same model, same tasks as E03 (collaborator's run from 2026-04-19). Result: paired delta -0.215 vs E03 (0.616 vs 0.831). Catastrophic regressions on the same 3 rescue tasks (Sneddon -0.735, DPWellbore -0.701, Mandel -0.610). MAJOR FINDING: cross-seed variance is huge (~0.2 mean delta), driven by the rescue tasks. The 'memory hurts -0.18 to -0.32' findings (E04-E13) are largely or entirely variance against the lucky E03 baseline. Memory variants score 0.62-0.65; plain plugin re-run scores 0.62.
decision_reason: Reframes all memory experiments. The plugin's headline +0.178 over no-plugin needs multi-seed verification. Memory variants are likely within noise of plugin baseline.

label: noplug_seed2: PLAIN no-plugin on 17 test (no-plugin variance anchor)
rationale: No-plugin cross-seed variance check on v1 specs. E15 mean 0.597 vs E01 mean 0.589 on 17 test - delta +0.008, within noise. No-plugin is highly stable across seeds (var <0.02). Confirms plugin-side cross-seed variance is asymmetric - plugin's rescue mechanism is variance-prone; no-plugin's consistent failure is low-variance.
decision_reason: Establishes that the plugin gain claim cannot be seed-variance-only; no-plugin's stability means any paired-same-seed plugin gain is real.

label: noplug_mm_v2: no-plugin + minimax + v2 specs on 17 test (canonical baseline)
rationale: Canonical baseline we never had. v2 specs = Brian's experiments_test36_template (the spec set that E03 was run on). 16/17 agent success, 12 scored (5 scoring errors from ParseError/RecursionError on no-plug-generated XML, not agent failures). Mean 0.566. With v2 specs + minimax this is the proper no-plugin reference.
decision_reason: Establishes the canonical no-plugin baseline on matched (v2) specs with the faster model we've chosen for future work.

label: plug_mm_v2_seed2: plain plugin + minimax + v2 specs seed 2 on 17 test
rationale: Plugin reproducibility + paired canonical baseline. 13/17 scored (4 failed_no_outputs unexpectedly - new failure mode on minimax). Mean 0.575, much lower than E02's 0.809 on 36 tasks. Cross-seed variance on plugin+minimax+v2 is ~0.23 similar to deepseek. Paired vs E16 (same seed): delta +0.055 - real but small plugin-only gain.
decision_reason: Matched-seed baseline for memory comparison. Shows plugin's cross-seed variance exists on minimax too. Plain-plugin canonical paired gain is only +0.055 vs no-plugin same seed.

label: gmemsilent_mm_v2: gmem-silent MCP + minimax + v2 specs on 17 test (MEMORY WINS)
rationale: First clean evidence memory helps. 17/17 success (vs E17's 13/17). Mean 0.725, median 0.847. Paired vs E16 (no-plug): delta +0.117. Paired vs E17 (plain plugin same seed): delta +0.094 - memory BEATS plain plugin. Wins 9/13 paired tasks. Biggest wins: IsothermalLeakyWell +0.511, CasedContactThermo +0.507, ModifiedCamClay +0.424, ThermoporoelasticConsolidation +0.298. Memory also prevents the plain-plugin failure modes (E17 had 4 failed_no_outputs; E18 had 0).
decision_reason: First clean positive memory result. Memory+plugin > plain plugin > no-plugin on canonical specs. Reframes all prior 'memory hurts' findings as spec-mismatch + variance artifacts. Motivates: re-test other memory variants on v2 specs + minimax, and multi-seed.

label: memshort_run1: plugin + short cross-task-only cheatsheet (~300 tokens) + explicit stop criterion on 17 test tasks
rationale: Isolates content-specificity (hypothesis 2) from context-bloat (hypothesis 1) for the E04 failure. New short cheatsheet: ~300 tokens, RAG usage + XML structure + common mistakes + explicit stop criterion. Result: 13/17 success (vs 17/17 plugin-only, matching E04's 13/17); mean TreeSim 0.561 on 14 scored vs 0.831 plugin-only on 17 (E03 test subset) - still catastrophically worse. Failure pattern identical to E04: same redacted_thinking -> end_turn signature on ModifiedCamClay (trapped in failed_no_outputs) and ExtendedDruckerPrager (new failure); and 2 timeouts (EDPWellbore, Mandel - both succeeded in both plugin-only and E04 long-cheatsheet conditions). Conclusion: length is NOT the dominant cause of cheatsheet failure. Any cheatsheet-in-system-prompt triggers the failure mode on a subset of tasks.
decision_reason: Confirms hypothesis 1 (context bloat alone) is insufficient to explain the E04 failure. Both long (1700t) and short (300t) cheatsheets show same failure pattern. Defers memory via system-prompt-inject design entirely; future memory should be MCP-tool-on-demand or on a different model.

label: mm_noplug_run1: minimax-m2.7 no-plugin on 17 test tasks (cross-model paired vs E02)
rationale: Cross-model robustness confirmed. 17/17 agent success in 17 min wall (workers=12, no rate-limit issues). Scored 15/17 (2 minimax tasks wrote malformed XML - ParseErrors on ExampleThermalLeakyWell and TutorialPoroelasticity). Paired on 15 tasks vs E02 (plugin+minimax): minimax no-plugin 0.694 vs minimax+plugin 0.809, delta -0.115 paired. Plugin wins 11/15, loses 4. Cross-model generalization of plugin advantage CONFIRMED on minimax-m2.7, though delta (-0.115) smaller than on deepseek-v3.2 (-0.175). Suggests plugin helps more on weaker base models.
decision_reason: Positive cross-model result. Strengthens paper claim: plugin advantage is not a deepseek artifact. Concurrency=12 workers confirmed safe for OpenRouter rate limits.

label: Stop-hook XML-verification rescue for minimax empty-completion failure
rationale: Prior session diagnosed failed_no_outputs on minimax/OpenRouter as provider-side empty completion after tool_result (XN-010); proposed plugin Stop hook to block end_turn when /workspace/inputs/ is missing XML. This session discovered the hook never loaded (schema bug + missing --plugin-dir); now fixed. Pre-campaign gate for E20 ablation.

label: Tool-list-shape confound for E18-vs-E17 rescue pattern
rationale: E18 (plug+gmem-silent) had 0 failed_no_outputs on the same 4 tasks that failed 4x in E17 (plug alone). Candidate mechanisms: (a) hook rescue [E18 had no hook], (b) any extra tool in list changes provider behavior, (c) memory tool docstring content, (d) single-run variance. Motivates C2 cell of E20.

label: plughook_mm_v2: plain plugin + Stop hook (planned, never ran due to silent hook-load failure)
rationale: Documented in misc/RUN_COMMANDS.md by prior session but never launched. Would have been a hook-off run indistinguishable from E17 because hooks.json schema was wrong and --plugin-dir was not passed to claude CLI. Superseded by E20.
decision_reason: Infrastructure never worked; subsumed by E20 narrow ablation with --settings-based hook routing.

label: E20 narrow hook-ablation: 4 E17-failure tasks × 3 independent runs × 4 cells (C0/C1/C2/C4)
rationale: Pre-registered design D-004. C0 (no hook) = E17 replicate; C1 (hook on) = rescue candidate; C2 (noop MCP, no hook) = tool-list confound; C4 (hook + noop) = interaction. Primary metric: failures-as-zero TreeSim, Wilcoxon paired. Smoketest verified plumbing 2026-04-21 11:40 (C2 mcp includes noop+connected; hook-events log fires).

label: E20 hook-ablation results (see XN-012)
rationale: 48 runs (4 tasks × 3 independent runs × 4 cells). E17's 4/17 failure rate did not reproduce — 0 failed_no_outputs in any cell. Paired Wilcoxon: hook effect Δ -0.112 fa0 TreeSim vs nohook (p≈0.31, n=12, underpowered). Runner retry salvaged 2 first-attempt failures that did fire. Hook validated as plumbing (fires, blocks, allows; event log works) but not as rescue mechanism. Decision per D-004 branch 4: do not expand.
decision_reason: Hook claim not supported. Ship hook as safety net, not as paper contribution. Reallocate time budget to fallback paths (multi-seed E18 replication; curl reproducer for true empty completions).

label: pac1_plug_hook_s1: plug + SR (Stop hook) + minimax + v2, 17 test tasks, seed 1
rationale: PAC-1 Phase A cell A3 (D-005). Full 17-task comparison complementing E20's narrow 4-task hook ablation. Pairs with E17 (plug-nohook) to attribute hook contribution over the full evaluation set. Uses claude_code_repo3_plugin default (hook ON via --settings).

label: pac1_plug_mem_hook_s1: FULL STACK (RAG+Memory+SR) + minimax + v2, 17 test tasks, seed 1
rationale: PAC-1 Phase A cell A5 (D-005) — headline full-stack condition. E18 ran before verify_outputs.py existed (hook files 12:08 UTC vs E18 completed 06:37 UTC) so E18 is effectively A4 not A5. E24 is the genuine full-stack run. Uses claude_code_repo3_plugin_gmemsilent with hook ON.

label: E23 pac1_plug_hook_s1 results: fa0 TreeSim 0.664 on 17 test tasks
rationale: PAC-1 A3 complete. 17/17 success. fa0 TreeSim 0.664. Hook fired 23 times: 16 xml_clean:allow, 4 no_xml block/rescue, 2 parse_error rescue, 1 parse_error_max_retries. 4 tasks genuinely rescued by hook (no_xml tasks: IsothermalLeakyWell 0.964, ThermalLeakyWell 0.381, Poroelasticity 0.448, Sneddon 0.128).
decision_reason: Single-seed positive delta vs baseline (+0.167) and vs E17 plug-alone (+0.225, diff seed). Confirm with seed 2.

label: E24 pac1_plug_mem_hook_s1 (FULL STACK) results: fa0 TreeSim 0.317 - STACK LOSES vs baseline
rationale: PAC-1 A5 complete. 17/17 success. fa0 TreeSim 0.317 - WORSE THAN BASELINE (-0.180 paired vs E16). Hook fired 28 times incl 9 no_xml blocks, 3 parse_error. Memory_lookup tool_use count: 0/17 (same as E18). Adding the memory tool to the tool list (uncalled) AND the hook produces catastrophic regression vs either alone. A5-A3 paired delta -0.347 on 17 tasks (3/13 W/L). The effect is NOT memory-content poisoning; it is a tool-list-shape interaction with the hook.
decision_reason: SURPRISE: full stack LOSES to baseline on single seed. Before any paper claim, need (a) seed 2 of A5 to rule out outlier, (b) A4' on current infra (no AQ) to isolate the hook's contribution from the AQ-removal confound. Multi-seed launched 2026-04-21T20:22 via /tmp/pac1_phase_a_seed2.sh.

label: PAC-1 Phase A findings: adversarial review skipped — codex CLI unavailable
rationale: codex CLI not installed on this workstation (same blocker as D-003 earlier today). Applied same-model self-critique as mitigation per skill attack-priority list. PAC-1 Phase A findings are explicitly single-seed and flagged preliminary; no paper claim yet. Multi-seed launched. Install codex before next paper-claim gate.

label: PAC-1 A3 seed 2: plug+SR (pac1_plug_hook_s2)
rationale: Multi-seed replication of A3. fa0 0.641 (seed 1 was 0.664). A3 cross-seed std ~0.017 — very stable. Confirms plug+SR is a reliable configuration.
decision_reason: A3 mean over 2 seeds = 0.653 vs baseline 0.497 = +0.155. Most stable positive cell.

label: PAC-1 A5 seed 2: full stack (pac1_plug_mem_hook_s2)
rationale: Multi-seed replication of A5. fa0 0.729 (seed 1 was 0.317). Reverses the seed-1 negative reading — seed 1 was an outlier. Confirms full stack is viable but highly seed-variable.
decision_reason: A5 now shows bimodal distribution across seeds (0.317, 0.729 — std 0.29). Next seed continues characterization.

label: PAC-1 A5 seed 3: full stack (pac1_plug_mem_hook_s3)
rationale: A5 third seed. fa0 0.776. Seeds 2 and 3 cluster at ~0.75; seed 1 (0.317) was outlier. A5 mean over 3 seeds = 0.607; std 0.252.
decision_reason: Full stack mean +0.110 over baseline, not yet statistically significant. A5 volatile vs A3 stable.

label: PAC-1 A4' seed 1: plug+Mem+nohook on current infra (pac1_plug_mem_nohook_s1)
rationale: Disambiguates A5-A4 confound. A4 (E18) ran with AskUserQuestion in tool list; current infra removed AQ. E25 = plug+mem-silent+no-hook on current infra. fa0 0.791. Confirms silent memory effect in absence of hook AND absence of AQ.
decision_reason: A4' seed 1 is highest single-seed fa0 in campaign. 2 seeds average 0.661 mean.

label: PAC-1 A4' seed 2: plug+Mem+nohook (pac1_plug_mem_nohook_s2)
rationale: Second seed of A4'. fa0 0.531 (2 parse errors dropped to 0). A4' n=2 mean 0.661 vs baseline 0.497 = +0.164; std 0.184 (high variance). Without hook, parse errors become 0s; hook's SR rescue is needed for variance reduction.
decision_reason: A4' confirms memory helps but is volatile without SR. Motivates keeping SR in the stack for reliability even if marginal TreeSim gain is zero.

label: A3 seed 3: plug+SR (pac1_plug_hook_s3)
rationale: Third A3 seed for proper baseline variance. fa0 0.267 — major outlier vs s1 (0.664) and s2 (0.641). A3 n=3 mean now 0.524, std 0.221 — much higher than n=2 σ=0.017 indicated. Confirms n=2 variance estimates unreliable (RN-003 P1 #4).
decision_reason: Memory ablation analysis must now use paired-per-task Wilcoxon, not mean+std.

label: D-007 memory ablation design (superseded by D-008)
rationale: Original design vetoed by RN-003: 4 P1 blockers including test-task GT filename leakage in memory_index.json (14/17 tasks affected).
decision_reason: Superseded by D-008.

label: Memory ablation V2: hygiene-fixed, placebo-controlled, cross-model distilled
rationale: Post-RN-003 fixes: hygiene audit gate, M-placebo control, token parity preregistered, gemini-3-flash-preview distillation, paired-per-task Wilcoxon. 6 new conditions × 3 seeds.
decision_reason: Active sprint — matrix launched 2026-04-22T04:29:36Z.
